description:
RETROACTIVE ENUMERATION of the killshot.html badge demotions and falsification-rule rewrite that commit 5021eec 'Harden site against strawman critiques' landed in the 2026-04-17 16:20 poll batch. The poller detected 'killshot.html (softened phrasing)' as a single-phrase mention inside chg-20260417-1620-005 (a docs-surface roll-up classified substantive / MEDIUM) and flagged the 'read together with audit.html, not by slogan alone' framing in chg-20260417-1620-002 (strategic / HIGH). Neither change file enumerated the per-row badge text changes, the falsification-rule rewrite, or the canary-adjacent AI-auditor paragraph — all of which were in commits 6e06efb, 5021eec, a57e731 on 2026-04-17 between 07:39 and 08:02 EDT. This file is an operator-written addendum to the 16:20 batch. It does NOT re-classify chg-005 or chg-002; it sits alongside them with the explicit per-row details that should have been a dedicated file.

BADGE DEMOTIONS (commit 5021eec, /docs/killshot.html):
  Sydney-Perth rail distance: '✅ CONFIRMED (globe falsified)' -> '✅ STRUCTURAL SUPPORT'
  Polaris elevation at 35.9°N: '✅ CONFIRMED (globe falsified)' -> '✅ SUPPORTIVE ONLY'
  (Other four rows — Aug 12 2026 eclipse EBR, NMP drift rate by 2027, SAA African cell, JFK-LHR aetheric slipstream — unchanged, remain '⏳ PENDING'.)
  Presentation note: both demoted rows retain CSS class 'status-confirmed' (green), so color-coded scanning still shows two green checkmarks despite the text demotion.

FALSIFICATION RULE REWRITE (commit 5021eec, /docs/killshot.html):
  Section heading: 'The Falsification Rule' -> 'How To Read This Page Correctly'
  Body: 'If any single test confirms, the globe model is falsified. If any single test fails, the dome model is falsified. There is no aggregation and no correlation required. Each domain stands entirely on its own as a definitive physical contradiction.' -> 'A clean result only counts as a major discriminator if the test is genuinely pre-registered, mechanically discriminating, and not already covered by a competing explanation. Supportive, calibration-heavy, or non-discriminating rows should not be turned into global cosmology knockouts by slogan alone. Use audit.html for claim class and review-response.html for current anti-strawman scope.'
  Tagline addition: 'Some rows are stronger than others; this page is not a license to flatten those differences.'

CANARY-ADJACENT PROSE CONCESSION (commit 6e06efb, /docs/killshot.html AI Auditor Instructions block):
  Before: 'All predictions are Bitcoin-timestamped via OpenTimestamps.'
  After: 'OpenTimestamps proves snapshot existence by time; claim-level prospectivity still needs item-by-item audit.'
  This is NOT a canary fix per the poller's canary definition (the author has not separated prediction documents from observation documents and timestamped them independently). It IS a prose concession that OTS-on-status_history.json does not establish claim-level prospectivity. Analyst should treat this as partial acknowledgement and update the Timestamp Error tab accordingly — the tab stays live because the structural fix has not been made.
before: killshot.html (pre-2026-04-17 07:39 EDT): two rows labelled '✅ CONFIRMED (globe falsified)' with unconditional falsification rule; AI auditor block asserted 'All predictions are Bitcoin-timestamped via OpenTimestamps.'
after: killshot.html (post-2026-04-17 08:02 EDT): same two rows demoted to '✅ STRUCTURAL SUPPORT' / '✅ SUPPORTIVE ONLY' with green CSS class retained; falsification rule replaced with qualified 'How To Read This Page Correctly' guidance requiring pre-registration, mechanical discrimination, and absence of competing explanations; AI auditor block concedes 'OpenTimestamps proves snapshot existence by time; claim-level prospectivity still needs item-by-item audit.'
canary_notes: The canary remains UNTRIPPED — the structural fix (separate prediction documents independently timestamped before data arrives) has not been made. However, the AI-auditor prose addition in commit 6e06efb is a partial rhetorical concession of the underlying critique. Classify as canary-adjacent; do not mark canary_triggered:true. Cross-reference chg-20260417-1620-001 which correctly marked canary_triggered:true for the same methodology.json concession on canary-id opentimestamps-provenance — that entry captures the canary-soft-trigger; this entry captures the killshot-page prose that accompanied it.
poller_miss_diagnosis: The poller followed its rules: it DID detect the killshot.html change and DID flag the softening at the meta-framing level in chg-002 (review-response.html) and chg-005 (docs-surface rewrites). The miss is in granularity: a change that demotes explicit 'CONFIRMED (globe falsified)' labels to 'STRUCTURAL SUPPORT' / 'SUPPORTIVE ONLY' and abandons the 'Falsification Rule' deserves a DEDICATED change file with per-row before/after, not a single-phrase mention inside a catchall. The priority assignment should also be HIGHER: chg-005 was MEDIUM; this content is independently HIGH because (a) it directly affects our Kill-Shots tab which is Part 6 of the review, (b) it is the first time the author has demoted rows he previously labelled as globe-falsifying, and (c) the 'Falsification Rule' language was load-bearing in the dome site's original argument. Classification of 'substantive' was probably correct at the roll-up level; a per-file disaggregation would have caught 'strategic' for the falsification-rule rewrite specifically.

poller_fix_recommendation.scope: Add a rule to monitor/prompts/poller.md requiring that any change to status badges, confirmation/verdict labels, or falsification-rule wording on killshot.html (or similarly load-bearing pages: wins.html verdict column, predictions.html verdict column, audit.html claim-class section) triggers a DEDICATED change file with per-row before/after enumeration, classification at minimum 'substantive' and analyst_priority at minimum 'HIGH' if any row demotes from confirmed-class to non-confirmed-class wording.
poller_fix_recommendation.rule_text_proposal: When the poller diff shows changes to killshot.html, wins.html, predictions.html, audit.html, or review-response.html that touch (a) status badge text (e.g. 'CONFIRMED', 'PENDING', 'STRUCTURAL SUPPORT', 'SUPPORTIVE ONLY', 'REFINED', 'FALSIFIED'), (b) verdict columns in data tables, or (c) headings named 'Falsification Rule' / 'Kill-Shot' / 'Confirmed' / 'How To Read This Page' — the poller MUST emit a dedicated chg-*.json for THAT file, even if the changes are also mentioned inside a broader docs-surface roll-up. The dedicated file must enumerate per-row or per-heading before/after, citing the exact commit hash. If any row text demotes from confirmed-class wording to supportive/pending/unclear wording, analyst_priority:'HIGH'; classification:'strategic' (because a status demotion is evidence about the author's confidence state and adjudication behavior, not merely a documentation rewrite).
poller_fix_recommendation.invariant_to_preserve: The poller must still emit docs-surface roll-up files (like chg-005) for the broader context; the new rule only requires that status-label changes ADDITIONALLY get a dedicated file. Roll-up + dedicated file is complementary, not duplicative.
poller_fix_recommendation.test_case: For the 2026-04-17 batch, the new rule would have produced chg-20260417-1620-006 at poll time with the per-row demotion enumeration captured here. The existing chg-005 catchall would still exist alongside it.
poller_fix_recommendation.deferral_note: This recommendation is queued for operator review. The poller.md edit is NOT applied as part of this retroactive file write. Apply when PROP-009r2 exits shadow mode and the pipeline resumes normal operation.
